briefs.episode_title: **Silver Price** New CRITICAL Facts YOU Must Hear - (Gold and Iran NEWS Update too)
briefs.one_line_summary: Escalating geopolitical tensions and supply constraints position silver and gold for a significant price surge.
core_claims: Silver is up $1.68/oz and gold is up $86/oz.
Trump projects the Middle East conflict could last four weeks.
Kuwait stock exchange halts trading amid US-Israel strikes on Iran.
An oil tanker sanctioned by the US was attacked in the Strait of Hormuz.
China may suspend gold trading due to price deviations and depleted liquidity.
The Comex saw 51 million ounces of silver drained in January and 48 million in February.
Silver mining is globally around 815 million ounces and not increasing.
Industrial silver use will increase due to solar panels, 5G, computer chips, AI, and EV batteries.
briefs.positioning_implication: Increase exposure to precious metals, anticipating significant market shifts driven by geopolitical instability and supply/demand imbalances.

briefs.episode_title: Why Every AI Skill You Learned 6 Months Ago Is Already Wrong (And What Is Replacing Them)
briefs.channel: AI News & Strategy Daily | Nate B Jones
briefs.one_line_summary: AI's continuous evolution renders previously learned skills obsolete, necessitating a shift towards continuous adaptation and boundary management.
core_claims: Every workforce skill, unlike AI, has historically had a defined endpoint.
The 'surface' of AI capability—the boundary between AI and human tasks—is where the most valuable work now lies, requiring skills in delegation, verification, and intervention.
AI's expanding capabilities cause tasks previously requiring human intervention to migrate inside the AI bubble, rendering prior skillsets obsolete.
As AI expands, the frontier of human-AI collaboration grows, demanding more human judgment, not less.
The critical skill is 'frontier operations': sensing, structuring, and maintaining a model of AI failure at the current edge.
briefs.positioning_implication: Shift workforce development away from fixed-destination training towards continuous calibration and boundary management skills. Prioritize developing 'frontier operations' capabilities to maintain relevance in the evolving AI landscape.

briefs.episode_title: Rick Rule Perfectly Called Silver Crash, Here’s What ‘Goes Crazy’ Next
briefs.one_line_summary: Shift from physical silver to silver stocks and broader macroeconomic concerns drive Rule's investment strategy.
core_claims: Most fiat currencies will decline by 75% over 10 years.
Silver price melt-up was an obvious precursor to a melt-down.
High-quality silver stocks were valued on Base Street with a $40-$45 price assumption.
If silver traded sideways, silver stocks could still go up by 50% in a year.
briefs.positioning_implication: Shift from physical assets to equities that offer potentially better risk-adjusted returns within the same sector; be prepared for fiat currency devaluation.

briefs.episode_title: URGENT: Gold & Silver Update! Key Indicators & Wartime Analysis
briefs.one_line_summary: Geopolitical instability is pushing gold and silver prices higher, requiring investors to prepare for potential all-time highs.
core_claims: Tether Gold is up 0.5% and Pax Gold is up 1.7% versus Friday's close, suggesting gold prices may increase when markets open.
Digital versions of gold and silver attract enthusiasts, while institutional investors heavily influence prices.
A military campaign lasting two to four weeks is expected before a ceasefire is reached in the Iran conflict.
Pax Gold traded up to $5,600 an ounce after the attack on Iran began but sold off, matching its intraday trading high from January 29th.
Core PCE inflation rose to 3% in the last reading, and producer prices rose 0.5% overall in January, potentially exceeding 6% annually.
Silver is breaking above the $92/oz resistance level, and $100 silver could occur in March alongside gold highs.
briefs.positioning_implication: Allocate capital to precious metals, but manage expectations for volatility; consider de-dollarization and central bank buying as key drivers.

briefs.episode_title: SPECIAL REPORT: US & Israel Now At War With Iran - What Will The Implications Be? | Ryan Bohl, RANE
briefs.one_line_summary: The US and Israel jointly struck Iran, killing its supreme leader, leading to retaliatory strikes across the region and heightened tensions.
core_claims: The US and Israel conducted a joint decapitation strike on Iran, killing Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and other senior officials.
The joint mission between the US and Israel was extensive, targeting many viable targets in a decapitation strike including political and kinetic control points.
A massive cyber attack was involved along with the suppression of air defenses.
Iran calculated their pre-planned retaliation and struck every single Gulf Cooperation Council country including Oman and Jordan and Israel.
CENTCOM confirmed that three US soldiers were killed, and five wounded, with casualties occurring throughout the GCC and within Israel, hundreds of casualties inside of Iran.
The strikes and retaliations will continue for several weeks, with plans for them to last up to 30 days contingent on various off-ramp scenarios.
briefs.positioning_implication: Assess geopolitical risk exposure; anticipate market volatility in energy and defense sectors.  Re-evaluate supply chain dependencies in the Middle East.

briefs.episode_title: Is Cohere the Next AI Powerhouse? | First Time Founders with Ed Elson
briefs.channel: The Prof G Pod – Scott Galloway
briefs.one_line_summary: Cohere focuses on enterprise-level AI solutions, distinguishing itself from consumer-centric AI companies.
core_claims: Cohere is valued at nearly $7 billion and is competing with giants like OpenAI and Anthropic.
There are only about 10 companies worldwide that can make foundational AI models.
Foundational models are the backbone of AI, powering systems for businesses and governments.
The enterprise market focus allows Cohere to tailor large language models for specific industry needs, such as secure deployment and efficient integration.
Synthetic data and chat finetuning are currently a key component of training models.
Focus on AGI distracts from the more pressing question of whether adequate policy is in place to encourage better income distribution.
briefs.positioning_implication: Assess the feasibility of AI solutions within enterprise environments, focusing on the nuanced needs and security requirements distinct from consumer applications.
